appDesc.message: Detects fake-interview malicious-repo traits when you open the GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket.

rule_RIG_016_title.message: Agent config hook runs on open (Claude/Gemini)

rule_RIG_020_title.message: AI rules file instructs agent to run a command

rule_RIG_017_title.message: AI instruction file hidden injection chars

rule_RIG_021_title.message: Dead-drop resolver (decoded string fetched as URL)

rule_RIG_024_title.message: Lifecycle script runs an in-repo script

rule_RIG_025_title.message: Obfuscated payload appended after a module export

d_RIG_001.message: Line $1 is $2 chars; the off-screen tail holds executable/network tokens

d_RIG_002.message: Line $1 hides code after $2 consecutive blanks

d_RIG_003.message: Spliced base64 reassembles and decodes to $1

d_RIG_007_var.message: Variable $1 from atob() is later executed

d_RIG_011.message: $1 script connects directly to an IP

d_RIG_012.message: $1 comes from a URL source in $2

d_RIG_013.message: Runs a task automatically when the folder opens

d_RIG_016.message: Hook runs on open (SessionStart) or runs a download/network/encoded/in-repo script

d_RIG_020.message: Rules file (alwaysApply) tells the agent to run a setup/in-repo script

d_RIG_017_tag.message: Contains invisible Unicode Tag chars (likely hidden prompt injection)

d_RIG_017_bidi.message: Contains bidirectional control chars (can hide reversed text)

d_RIG_017_zw.message: Contains a run of zero-width chars (possible hidden payload)

d_RIG_021.message: A base64/atob-decoded string is fetched directly as a URL (dead-drop)

d_RIG_022.message: A network response is passed to eval()/Function()

d_RIG_023.message: The whole process.env is sent to a network sink

d_RIG_024.message: $1 script runs an in-repo script from a non-build path

d_RIG_025.message: Obfuscated executable code is appended after the module export (injected payload)

d_RIG_026.message: A script rewrites git history and tampers with the system clock (anti-forensic)

ui_head_alarm_repo.message: This repo's config files show malicious-repo traits

ui_head_caution.message: Suspicious traits detected - worth a look

ui_ai_check.message: Copy traits for your trusted AI to verify ⧉

ui_ai_check_done.message: Copied - paste it into your trusted AI ✓

llm_intro.message: While browsing code, a tool that detects malicious-repo traits (obfuscated code, hidden C2, run-on-open configs, info-stealer paths) flagged the following, but it may be a false positive. Please help me judge whether it is genuinely suspicious:

llm_questions.message: Please explain: 1) what these traits actually do; 2) whether they match info-stealing / C2 / run-on-open malicious behavior, or are common legitimate uses; 3) your risk judgment and advice.

pp_na_sub.message: Metsuke runs on GitHub / GitLab / Bitbucket file pages and repo home

pp_unreadable_repo_sub.message: This repo may be private or lacks these config files

pp_unreadable_file_sub.message: Not analyzed, to avoid false positives

pp_clean_repo_sub.message: Scanned $1 entry files, no traits found
